front: What are microRNAs and how do they regulate peptide expression?
back: MicroRNAs (miRNAs, ~22 nt) bind 3'UTR of mRNAs, causing translational repression or mRNA degradation. They regulate: 1) Peptide precursor expression, 2) Processing enzyme levels, 3) Receptor expression, 4) Signal transduction pathways. miRNA mimic/antagonist therapeutics are being developed.
